message:
Fix SQL injection, WAL compatibility, agent dedup, and metric safety

Broad hardening pass across CLI, database, and tools layers addressing
security, correctness, and robustness issues:

Security:
- mail_explorer.rs: fix SQL injection in resolve_agent_ids by replacing
  format string interpolation with parameterized ?2 binding
- migrate.rs: parameterize sqlite_master table existence check

Database correctness:
- robot.rs: replace SQL NOT EXISTS subquery for agent dedup with
  post-query HashSet dedup — simpler, avoids complex correlated
  subquery that was fragile across SQLite dialects
- pool.rs: change u64 overflow fallback from MAX to 0 for pool stats
  (MAX would corrupt gauge metrics); fix pragma check to accept
  multi-row all-ok responses instead of requiring exactly one row
- coalesce.rs: move evidence_ledger().record() calls into individual
  outcome branches (Joined, Timeout fallback, LeaderFailed fallback)
  so each outcome is logged with its actual disposition

CLI & compatibility:
- lib.rs (doctor): add frankensqlite WAL compatibility handling —
  rusqlite cannot read frankensqlite WAL format, so treat
  "database disk image is malformed" from compatibility_probe as
  acceptable; add WAL checkpoint + sidecar cleanup in test setup
- metrics.rs: clamp latency_delta_us with saturating_add before
  cast to u64 to prevent underflow on extremely negative deltas

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -471,9 +471,7 @@ fn probe_via_curl(parsed: &ParsedUrl, config: &ProbeConfig) -> Result<RawRespons
             "-", // dump headers to stdout
             "--max-time",
             &timeout_secs.to_string(),
-            "-L", // follow redirects (curl handles them)
-            "--max-redirs",
-            "0", // but we handle redirects ourselves
+            // Do not follow redirects — we handle them ourselves
             &url,
         ])
         .output()
